source: U.S. Census Bureau, ACS 5-Year Estimates 2023, Table B11003
table_title: Family Type by Presence and Age of Own Children Under 18 Years

B11003_009E.label: Male HH: With own children under 18

B11003_011E.label: Male HH: Under 6 AND 6 to 17 years

B11003_013E.label: Male HH: No own children under 18

B11003_014E.label: Female householder, no husband present (need full pull)

B11003_015E.label: Female HH: With own children under 18 (derived)

analysis_note: B11003 reports presence/age of children, NOT number of children per family. Cannot directly derive multi-child family rate from this table. The 'Under 6 AND 6-17' rows (B11003_005, B11003_011) confirm multi-child families spanning age bands exist, but the total multi-child rate requires a different table (e.g., B09002, detailed household tables). The national SIPP-based estimate of 30-40% multi-child families remains the best available proxy.

partial_multi_child_floor.note: These are families confirmed to have children in BOTH <6 and 6-17 age groups — a floor on multi-child families, not the total count
